title: Ashwagandha
author: Nirogyn Editorial
content:
Introduction to Ashwagandha

Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) is one of the most revered herbs in Ayurvedic medicine, traditionally used to support stress resilience, energy, and hormonal balance.

Key Benefits

  • Stress and anxiety reduction
  • Cognitive function support
  • Sleep quality improvement

title: Vitamin D3
author: Nirogyn Editorial
content:
Introduction to Vitamin D3

Vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol) is a fat-soluble vitamin that plays a crucial role in immune function, bone health, and mood regulation.

Key Benefits

  • Immune system support
  • Bone health
  • Mood regulation

title: Lutein
author: Nirogyn Editorial
content:
Introduction to Lutein

Lutein is a carotenoid antioxidant found naturally in the macula of the eye. It acts as a natural sunscreen, protecting the eyes from harmful blue light and oxidative stress.

Key Benefits

  • Eye Health: Helps protect against age-related macular degeneration and cataracts.
  • Skin Health: Antioxidant properties benefit the skin by protecting against UV damage.

title: Chia Seeds
author: Nirogyn Editorial
content:
Introduction to Chia Seeds

Chia seeds (Salvia hispanica) are tiny black and white seeds that pack an impressive nutritional punch with fiber, omega-3 fatty acids, and minerals.

title: Magnesium Citrate
author: Nirogyn Editorial
content:
Introduction to Magnesium Citrate

Magnesium is an essential mineral involved in over 300 biochemical reactions in the body, supporting sleep, muscle function, and stress response.

title: Psyllium Husk
author: Nirogyn Editorial
content:
Introduction to Psyllium Husk

Psyllium husk is a type of soluble fiber derived from the seeds of the Plantago ovata plant, widely used to support digestive health and regularity.
